hero.thesis: Operate a small bulk vending machine route by placing 4–8 machines in high-traffic locations like offices and laundromats. Each machine can generate $50–$150 monthly with minimal upkeep, providing a passive-ish income stream.
hero.marketSize: The US bulk vending market is worth $3 billion, with growth driven by low entry barriers and the demand for convenience.
hero.payingEvidence: Businesses like 'VendWorks' and 'SnackStream' have achieved success by placing machines in strategic locations, proving consistent revenue streams.

actions.action: Research local businesses with high foot traffic using Google Maps and LinkedIn.
Join forums like 'VendingRoutePros' and engage with local operators for insights.
Cold call 5 potential locations to gauge interest in vending machines.

ninetyDay.title: Vendor Selection & Machine Procurement

actions.action: Contact 3 bulk vending machine suppliers for quotes and specifications.
Research vending machine models on 'MachineMart' and 'VendSource'.
Purchase 4 machines from preferred supplier.

ninetyDay.metric: 4 machines purchased and scheduled for delivery.

actions.action: Survey potential customers at chosen locations about preferred snack items.
Purchase initial stock from wholesaler 'SnackSupplyCo'.
Stock machines at secured locations.
actions.success: Machines stocked and ready for use.

ninetyDay.title: Launch & Early Customer Engagement

actions.action: Install machines at locations and conduct final checks.
Create and distribute flyers at machine locations to announce availability.
Check sales data from machines to assess initial performance.

ninetyDay.metric: First sales made from each machine.

ninetyDay.title: Performance Review & Fine-Tuning

actions.action: Analyze sales data to identify top-performing products.
Restock machines with top-selling products.
Reach out to location managers for feedback.

ninetyDay.metric: Increased sales by 20% from initial data.

ninetyDay.title: Expansion Planning

actions.action: Identify 5 new potential locations based on current success metrics.
Contact existing supplier for additional machine pricing.
Draft plan for scaling to 8 machines.

ninetyDay.metric: Expansion plan ready with potential new locations.

ninetyDay.title: Community Engagement & Brand Building

actions.action: Engage with local business groups on platforms like 'BizLinkLocal'.
Post success stories and customer testimonials on social media.
Organize a small promotional event at a high-performing location.

ninetyDay.metric: Increased brand recognition and social media engagement.

actions.action: Set up financial tracking using 'QuickBooks' to monitor income and expenses.
Review monthly financial reports to identify cost-saving opportunities.
Implement identified cost-saving measures.

ninetyDay.title: Feedback Loop & Continuous Improvement

actions.action: Implement a feedback system for location managers and customers.
Analyze feedback and adjust product offerings accordingly.
Monitor changes in sales post-adjustment.

ninetyDay.metric: Improved customer satisfaction and sales metrics.

actions.action: Negotiate placement agreements with new identified locations.
Order additional machines and products based on expansion plan.
actions.success: Order confirmed for new machines and stock.

actions.action: Install and stock new machines.
Develop a loyalty program for frequent customers.
Launch a targeted social media campaign to increase awareness.
Evaluate campaign performance metrics.

ninetyDay.title: Long-Term Strategy & Automation

actions.action: Evaluate potential for automation tools like 'Make' for restocking alerts.
Plan for semi-automated restocking process implementation.
Set long-term goals for further scaling and revenue targets.

ninetyDay.metric: Established semi-automated processes and long-term strategy.

toolStack.name: LinkedIn
toolStack.why: Connect with local business owners and managers.

toolStack.name: SnackSupplyCo
toolStack.why: Source bulk inventory at wholesale prices.

firstTenCustomers.script: Hi [Name], I'm [Your Name] and I specialize in placing high-quality vending machines in strategic locations. I've identified [Niche] as a perfect fit for our machines due to its high foot traffic. Can we schedule a quick call to discuss potential benefits for your business?
firstTenCustomers.cadence: Initial contact on Monday, follow-up Wednesday, final reminder Friday.

pricing.raisingRules: Review pricing every 6 months based on demand and operational costs.

failureModes: Poor location choice: Low foot traffic.
Lack of diversification: Limited product range.

setup.llc: Use Stripe Atlas to incorporate your business as an LLC for liability protection and tax benefits.
setup.payments: Set up with Stripe for easy payment processing and financial tracking.
setup.contracts: Draft simple placement agreements with location owners to define terms and responsibilities.
setup.insurance: Get a general liability insurance policy to cover potential damages or incidents.

scaling: To scale your vending machine route, consider hiring part-time help for machine restocking and maintenance. This frees up your time for strategic tasks like securing new locations and expanding product offerings. Use 'Make' to automate inventory alerts and streamline operations. As you grow, invest in a CRM to manage customer relationships and a more robust financial tracking system like 'QuickBooks'. Consider expanding into new territories or diversifying product ranges to include healthier options or seasonal items, enhancing appeal and boosting revenue.
